### Post by Titan8990 on 2008-07-26
After my upgrade to Hardy my monitor is no longer detected and I am unable to select my native resolution of 1920x1200. I tried to edit xorg.conf but the resolution that I added still is not an option in the screen resolution GUI and the entry that I removed is still an option.

Here are the contents:

```

---

### Post by ProbablyX on 2008-07-26
Run:
```

sudo mv /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf.backup
sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg

```

That should re-detect and create a new xorg.conf. If not you have a backup of the old one as created on the first line,

---

### Post by northern lights on 2008-07-26
First, let's backup your current xorg.conf```
cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.backup
```

Now, let's reconfigure the xserver:

Run ```
sudo /etc/init.d/gdm stop
``` from a terminal. You will be leaving the graphical environment, so remember the next two steps!

Press Alt+F1 to log in and execute ```
sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
```

Then restart the interface with ```
sudo /etc/init.d/gdm start
```

Try to change resolution again.

In case something gets (even more) screwed, revert back with```
cp /etc/X11/xorg.backup /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```

---
